meta.subject: Zambian Languages
meta.philosophy: The Zambian Languages Upper Primary School Syllabus has been developed to facilitate learning that will promote competences in learners from Grade 4 to 6. It has been designed to equip learners with skills to access, criticise, analyse and practically apply knowledge. It provides content which should allow learners to acquire more advanced competences in listening and speaking, reading fluency, vocabulary use, writing and language structure. It also provides topics and subtopics on customs and traditions because Zambian Languages also carry the culture of the Zambian people. Zambian Languages are vehicles through which most Zambians communicate with each other. Therefore, it is important that learners learn Zambian Languages in order to know how to use the language correctly and appropriately as well to appreciate the technical aspects of the language which cannot be acquired outside formal instruction. Teaching and learning Zambian Languages will enable learners to eventually become experts in translation, interpretation, writing, editing and publishing among others. It will also act as a resource for cultural preservation and increase learners' opportunities for further education. The content is packaged to stimulate learners in thinking reflectively, logically, creatively, and critically so as to promote the formation of positive social behaviour. The activities provided are aimed at engaging learners in linguistic practice thereby fostering proficiency.
meta.competence_learning: The syllabus is divided into six (6) components: listening and speaking, reading, vocabulary, comprehension, writing and language structure. Segmenting the syllabus into these components provides a convenient way of organising the syllabus. However, at lesson delivery, the teacher should ensure that necessary components are integrated into one lesson. Each of the six components is further divided into topic, sub-topic, specific competence, learning activities and expected standard. Each subtopic has one or more specific competences which learners are expected to acquire. Similarly, each specific competence has one or more suggested activities which learners should engage in. The expected standard provides a criterion upon which the teacher will base his/her judgement of the learners' performance to that particular activity. Teachers are encouraged to scaffold struggling learners so that they can also meet the expected standard. The 2023 Zambia Education Curriculum Framework has outlined twelve competences that learners should attain at the end of Upper Primary in Zambian Languages.
goals: Nurture effective communication both in speech and writing.
Develop as individuals and eventually contribute to the wellbeing of society and the nation as a whole.
Become experts in translation, interpretation, writing, editing and publishing among others.
Act as a resource for cultural preservation and increase learners' opportunities for further education.
Promote competences in learners to access, criticise, analyse and practically apply knowledge.
Equip learners with skills to access, criticise, analyse and practically apply knowledge.
Acquire more advanced competences in listening and speaking, reading fluency, vocabulary use, writing and language structure.
Understand and appreciate customs and traditions as Zambian Languages carry the culture of the Zambian people.
Promote the formation of positive social behaviour.
Foster linguistic proficiency through engaging learners in linguistic practice.

specific_outcomes: Narrate fiction and non- fiction stories
Ask for and provide information on directions
Use appropriate language to express different emotions
Give oral reports on witnessed events
Demonstrate understanding of cultural norms and values on respect
Give meanings of words from a given context
Identify colours in a Zambian Language
Listen to information read out or told aloud
Read different types of materials for different purposes
Arrange sentences in logical order to form a paragraph
Translate words and sentences from English into a Zambian Language
Change sentences from one tense to another
Construct sentences using Countable and Uncountable Nouns
Distinguish meaning of words by vowel length
Identify and use adverbs of place
Identify and use adverbs of time
